title: Bonus Tax Relief for America’s Seniors Act
short_title: Bonus Tax Relief for America’s Seniors Act
plain_summary: This bill helps older Americans pay less in taxes. Right now, seniors get an extra six hundred dollar tax deduction, but this bill would increase it to five thousand dollars. The new amount would start in 2026 and would go up each year to keep up with rising prices. This means seniors would keep more of their money instead of paying it in taxes.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Ways and Means.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Bonus Tax Relief for America's Seniors Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Increase in Additional Standard Deduction for Seniors
sections.text: This section changes the tax code to give seniors a bigger tax break when they file their taxes. Right now, people who are sixty five years old or older get an extra six hundred dollars taken off their taxable income. This law would increase that amount to five thousand dollars. Starting in twenty twenty seven, this five thousand dollar amount will go up each year to keep up with the cost of living. The government will use the same formula it uses for other tax adjustments to figure out how much to increase it. If the increase is not an even fifty dollar amount, it will be rounded down to the nearest fifty dollars. These changes will start for tax years beginning after December thirty first, twenty twenty five. The law also makes small changes to other parts of the tax code to make sure everything works together properly.
